incident_details_required.incident_location: Rear yards between 742 and 746 East Willow Lane, Springfield, IL 62704
incident_details_required.other_notes_line_1: For the past six months, the neighbor’s two large dogs have been barking loudly and persistently late at night and very early in the morning, often for 30–45 minutes at a time, several times per night. The barking is typically continuous, high-volume, and occurs even when no one appears to be outside interacting with them.
incident_details_required.other_notes_line_2: On 2025-12-08, the barking started around 11:15 pm and continued almost nonstop until shortly after midnight. My husband Marcus and I had to be up at 5:30 am for work, and our 10-year-old daughter, Emily, was awakened twice and came into our bedroom crying because she was scared and could not fall back asleep. This has become a regular pattern on weeknights and weekends, and we are all experiencing ongoing sleep disruption, headaches, and difficulty concentrating at work and school.
incident_details_required.other_notes_line_3: We have spoken with the owner, Daniel Brooks, on three separate occasions (in early August, late September, and again in mid-November). Each time he acknowledged the issue but has not made any lasting changes. The dogs are often left outside unattended for long periods, especially after 10:00 pm, even in cold weather. We have tried using white noise machines and closing all windows, but the barking is still loud enough to be clearly heard inside our home. Our adjacent neighbor, Patricia Gomez at 738 East Willow, has also been disturbed and has agreed to be listed as an additional complainant. We are filing this complaint as a last resort in hopes that Animal Control can help ensure the dogs are managed in a way that allows our families to sleep through the night.

description_of_animals.animal_color_row_1: Black and tan with a small white patch on chest
description_of_animals.animal_name_row_1: "Ranger" (name shouted by owner when calling him inside)
